new_tenders.solicitation_title: W0134-26R016 - Aviator’s Breathing Oxygen and Liquid Nitrogen with…
new_tenders.gsin_description: Please note that late submissions will not be considered or accepted. The Department of National Defence (DND), Canadian Forces Base (CFB) Cold Lake, 4 Wing, requires the supply and delivery of liquid Aviator’s Breathing Oxygen (ABO) and liquid nitrogen (LN₂), as required. This requirement includes the provision, installation, operation, and management of contractor owned on site storage tanks for liquid oxygen (LOX) and liquid nitrogen (LN₂), including all associated systems. This solicitation of offers is open only to Canadian suppliers and to Suppliers of an Applicable Trading Partner. An optional site visit will be held on April 22, 2026, beginning at 1:30PM MT. Canada recommends that the Offeror attend the site visit. To attend the site visit, Offerors must communicate with the Standing Offer Authority no later than April 6, 2026 at 4:00PM MT to confirm attendance and provide the required information of their representatives in order to gain entry to CFB 4Wing. Please see 5.2. Site Visit. on page 9 of the RFSO for full registration information. Offerors who do not attend will not be given an alternative appointment but they will not be precluded from submitting an offer.
new_tenders.client: Department of Public Works and Government Services (PSPC)

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Dental laboratory services
new_tenders.gsin_description: Dental Laboratory Services This requirement is for: The Correctional Service of Canada, Quebec Region Trade agreement: This procurement is not subject to any trade agreement. Policy on Reciprocal Procurement: This solicitation is open only to Canadian suppliers Tendering procedures: All interested suppliers may submit a bid. Competitive Procurement Strategy: lowest priced compliant bid Set-aside under the Procurement Strategy for Indigenous Business: This procurement is not subject to any set-asides for Indigenous Suppliers. Comprehensive Land Claim Agreement: This procurement is not subject to a Comprehensive Land Claims Agreement. Security Requirements: This contract includes security requirements. Nature of Requirements: The following is a summary of the statement of work for this requirement. Correctional Service Canada (CSC) is obliged to provide, on an as-needed basis, dental laboratory services to offenders residing in federal institutions in the Quebec Region. The work will include the following: Objectives: The Contractor must provide essential dental laboratory services, including but not limited to the fabrication, repair, and relining of full dental prosthetics (upper, lower, or both) and partial prosthetics, in accordance with the laws, regulations, and standards governing the practice of dental technologists and dental appliances. The Contractor must perform all work in accordance with the following standards: a. Practices of dental prosthesis and appliance technologists; b. Professional standards of the Ordre des technologues en prothèses et appareils dentaires du Québec as well as the Ordre des Denturologistes du Québec; and c. CSC policies, standards, and guidelines that apply to federal institutions in the Quebec Region. Deliverables: The Contractor must provide the following deliverables: a. Pink acrylic dental prostheses that are new, repaired, rebased, relined or a combination of these services, as needed. b. A complete delivery of dental prosthetics within a maximum of ten business days from the receipt of the request. c. The containers needed for transporting prosthetics. Any conditions for participation of suppliers not specified in solicitation documentation: none. Estimated quantity of commodity: see solicitation document statement of work and basis of payment. Duration of Contract and Time Frame for Delivery: Period of the Contract: The Work is to be performed from date of Contract to three (3) years later inclusive File Number: 21120-26-5167826/A Contracting Authority: Nassima Aliouat Telephone number: 343-540-8957 E-mail: Nassima.aliouat@csc-scc.gc.ca NOTE TO BIDDERS: Bidders can obtain the complete statement of work and evaluation criteria by downloading the solicitation document and associated documents from the Canada buys / tender opportunities website. The Crown reserves the right to negotiate with suppliers on any procurement. Documents may be submitted in either official language of Canada (English or French). After contract award, bidders may request a debriefing on the results of the bid solicitation process. Bidders should make the request to the Contracting Authority within fifteen (15) working days of receipt of the results of the bid solicitation process. The debriefing may be in writing, by telephone or in person. Procurement Assistance Canada (PAC) offers seminars to businesses interested in learning how to sell goods and services to the Government of Canada. The seminars are FREE. Topics include:  Overview of the federal government contracting process;  Searching for opportunities on the Buy and Sell - Tenders website;  Bidding on opportunities;  Registering in supplier databases. The full schedule of seminars can be found on the Buyandsell.gc.ca website, under Event Calendar (https://buyandsell.gc.ca/event-calendar).

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Radar Beacons (RACONs)
new_tenders.gsin_description: Please note that late submissions will not be considered or accepted. CCG has a requirement to provide Radar Beacons (RACONs) to the Canadian Coast Guard to support its Floating Aids to Navigation (AtoN) Program for the purpose of providing safety in marine transportation and recreational boating. A RACON is a transmitter-receiver, when triggered by a radar, automatically returns a distinctive signal which can provide range, bearing and identification information. The systems will have to operate in all seasons for Canadian waterways and coastal waters. This notice is published on CanadaBuys for a period of 31 calendar days. The period of the Contract is from the date of Contract award to November 30, 2027 inclusive. One additional one year option period may be exercised to extend the contract from December 1, 2027 to November 30, 2028 inclusive.
new_tenders.client: Department of Public Works and Government Services (PSPC)

new_tenders.solicitation_title: 2025 ROADS REHABILITATION & MAINTENANCE
new_tenders.gsin_description: The Cape Breton Regional Municipality (CBRM) requires Contractors to perform road rehabilitation and maintenance activities on behalf of the Engineering Services and Public Works Departments. Activities include, but are not limited to, milling and paving asphalt, subgrade removal and preparation, concrete rehabilitation, miscellaneous asphalt and concrete repairs, and all traffic accommodation. The size and complexity of this work can vary throughout the duration of the 2026 construction season. This RFSQ will form a non-binding contract with the successful contractors to develop a Roster of Contractors to complete the associated work. Successful Contractors will sign a Master Agreement and become an eligible participant in the Call-Up Process. CBRM will then issue Statements of Requirements outlining the specific project details. new_tenders.solicitation_title: Peace River School Division - Invitation to Bid - 2026 PRSD (RFT) Supply…
new_tenders.gsin_description: Scope of Tender: 70-Passenger School Buses The Peace River School Division (PRSD) invites tenders for the supply of seven (7) new 70-passenger Type C school buses. Supply Details Powertrain Options: Vendors are encouraged to submit pricing for both gasoline and diesel options. PRSD will determine the final powertrain selection based on a thorough review of all submissions. Standards: Units must meet current CSA D250 standards and be configured for Northern Alberta cold-weather operations. Delivery: FOB Grimshaw, Alberta. Submission Requirements All submissions must include the following: Completed & Signed Specification and Pricing Checklist: Ensure all technical confirmations are signed. Pricing Submission: Clearly specify if units are "In Stock" or "Factory Order." Technical Documentation: Required for any specification exceptions. Key Information Quantity: 7 Units Closing Date: April 17, 2026, at 12:00 PM Evaluation: Tenders will be assessed on technical compliance, safety features, warranty, delivery schedule, and overall value. Important Note: PRSD is not obligated to accept the lowest-priced tender. Trade Agreements: This procurement is subject to CFTA and NWPTA. Notice: This summary does not supersede the detailed specifications. Vendors are responsible for reviewing the full Tender Documents and Appendices attached below.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: RFP# 26-12 Supply & Delivery of Janitorial Supplies
new_tenders.gsin_description: This Request for Proposals (the “RFP”) is an invitation by the Cowichan Valley Regional District (the “CVRD”) to prospective proponents to submit proposals for Supply & Delivery of Janitorial Supplies, as further described in Section A of the RFP Particulars (Appendix D) (the “Deliverables”). This RFP is for Supply and Delivery only and does not include janitorial cleaning services. The CVRD is inviting proposals from experienced and qualified janitorial and cleaning supply providers who offer a full range of commercial janitorial supplies, paper towels, toilet tissue and corresponding dispensers to be shipped to multiple delivery locations on an “as and when required” basis. Notes This will be the only notice posted to BC Bid concerning this project. All documents/addenda are only available for download/submission on the CVRD Procurement Portal Bonfire. It is the sole responsibility of the Respondent to check the Bonfire portal for any addenda(s) before submitting their response. All awards and results will be posted on CVRD Procurement Portal Bonfire https://cvrd.bonfirehub.ca/portal/?tab=pastOpportunities.  Contact Purchasing at Purchasing@cvrd.bc.ca should you have any further questions.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: W0113-26CS14 Vehicle Maintenance and Repairs – MSVS MILCOTS
new_tenders.gsin_description: AMENDMENT TO CLOSING TIME: Please disregard the Ariba Discovery posting response deadline closing time and CanadaBuys posting closing time. All required supporting documentation and bid submission must be submitted by 2026-04-21 at 14:00 EDT. All bids submitted after 14:00 EDT will result in the bid being declared non-responsive. Canada is seeking offers from Offerors to provide maintenance and repairs of Medium Support Vehicle System (MSVS) Militarized Commercial off the Shelf (MILCOTs). The requirement is “as and when required” for the provision of all labour, parts, and equipment necessary for the diagnostic, repair, and refurbishment services of the MSVS MILCOT – Navistar Internationals 7400 (SFA 6x6) and/or International WorkStar from 2009 to Department of National Defence (DND), Canadian Forces Base (CFB) Borden, Technical Services. The requirement includes two (2) streams (Stream 1 All Suppliers and Stream 2 Procurement Strategy for Indigenous Business). Streams will be evaluated separately, with the lowest priced offer for each stream issued a Standing Offer.
